channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Cracks emerge in House GOP after speaker's threat to saddle California wildfire aid with conditions
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — California Republicans are pushing back against suggestions by President Donald Trump, House Speaker Mike Johnson and other Republicans that federal disaster aid for victims of wildfires that ravaged Southern California should come with strings attached, possibly jeopardizing the
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firefighters battle to maintain the upper hand on a huge fire north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters fought to maintain the upper hand on a huge and rapidly moving wildfire that swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles and resulted in more than 50,000 people being put under evacuation orders or warnings.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a new contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7 police officers wounded in Texas shooting
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Seven San Antonio police officers were shot Wednesday night while responding to a “suicide in progress” call and a heavy presence of police were near an apartment complex trying to detain the suspect, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to protect your home and belongings in case of a fire or emergency
item.description: Ken Maccarone joins "Morning in America" to learn vital strategies to protect yourself and your loved ones during a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Ken Maccarone, NewsNation

item.title: The Gulf of Whatnow? Mapmakers grapple with Trump's geographic renaming plans
item.description: Mapmakers and teachers are re-thinking what to call the gulf of water between Mexico, the United States and Cuba after President Donald Trump ordered it renamed from the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Laurie Kellman And Hillel Italie, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria Wednesday left a female student dead and another student wounded, nearly two years after another deadly school shooting in the city that ignited an emotional debate about gun control in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A bid to block Trump's cancellation of birthright citizenship is in federal court
item.description: A federal judge is set to hear the first arguments in a multi-state lawsuit seeking to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents' immigration status.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sundance 2025: 13(ish) movies to look forward to
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — The Sundance Film Festival catalogue can be overwhelming to navigate, with around 90 feature films playing across 11 days.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Oscar nominations are Thursday. Here's what to look for
item.description: In the wake of devastating wildfires in Los Angeles that struck at the heart of the movie industry, the twice-delayed nominations to the 97th Academy Awards are going forward Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump taps Sean Curran as next Secret Service director
item.description: Curran was part of Trump's security details during an attempted assassination in Butler, Pennsylvania, last July.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Sonja Stafford, 60, is charged with 62 criminal counts including neglect of a dependent, human labor trafficking, strangulation and more.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, NewsNation

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look for favorable jurors on charges he fired gun at former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors packed into a Los Angeles courtroom again on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vornado space heaters recalled for potential shock and fire hazards
item.description: The potential danger stems from power cords that may become partially detached from the heater enclosure.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Fire chief shot, killed after stopping to help driver who struck a deer in Alabama
item.description: “We lost one of our brothers, leaders, mentor, and family to a senseless tragedy,” Cauthen's colleagues wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, Fox 5

item.title: Fast-moving Hughes Fire erupts in Los Angeles County as California officials order evacuations
item.description: Officials ordered evacuations in the area surrounding the Hughes Fire on Wednesday morning, located near Castaic Lake in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas man who tried to scam ex-US Rep. George Santos sentenced to 18 months in prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Texas man was sentenced to a year and a half in prison Wednesday over his effort to dupe George Santos out of nearly $1 million by offering to destroy evidence in the disgraced congressman’s corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Pet tortoise reunited with family after 'miraculous' fire survival
item.description: "Rocky had been hibernating in her burrow and miraculously survived the blaze!," Amanda Williams, whose uncle is Rocky's owner, wrote.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, ABC 4

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Everything you need to know about the 2025 Sundance Film Festival
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — As many in the entertainment industry navigate the devastating effects of the California wildfires, some will soon be decamping to cleaner air in the mountains. The annual Sundance Film Festival begins Thursday in Park City, Utah.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico setting up tents for migrants deported by Trump
item.description: Mexico on Tuesday continued to prepare for President Donald Trump’s promised mass deportations by setting up the first of nine migrant attention centers on the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: "I hereby order the Secretary of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administrator to immediately return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring, as required by law," the directive read.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Border security is popular – but that may be the limit of US immigration consensus: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults are on board with the idea of beefing up security at the southern border and undertaking some targeted deportations, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: Florida sees heavy snowfall during winter storm
item.description: Florida isn't usually a state that sees much snow, but that changed Tuesday as Winter Storm Enzo blew through, covering roadways in snowfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Closing arguments begin in federal corruption trial of Michael Madigan
item.description: The original 106 page 22 count indictment accused the once-powerful leader of acting like an organized crime boss.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration, according to a memo to the workforce obtained by
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has moved to end affirmative action in federal contracting and directed that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent was shot and killed in northern Vermont on Monday afternoon, the FBI said in a statement later that evening.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4
